questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes reflection of light?

options.A: Bending of light when it passes through water
options.B: Bouncing back of light from a surface
options.C: Absorption of light by an object

questions.explanation: Reflection is the bouncing back of light from a surface such as a mirror.

questions.question: What type of image is formed by a plane mirror?
questions.explanation: Plane mirrors form virtual and upright images.

questions.question: Why do convex mirrors provide a wider field of view?
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors diverge light rays, allowing a larger area to be seen.

questions.question: Which mirror can form both real and virtual images depending on the object position?
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors can form real or virtual images depending on object distance.

questions.question: Which device uses two plane mirrors to allow viewing objects from hidden positions?
questions.explanation: Periscopes use two plane mirrors to reflect light to the observer.

questions.question: What happens to light rays when they pass through a convex lens?
questions.explanation: Convex lenses converge parallel light rays to a focal point.

questions.question: Which lens is used in spectacles to correct long-sightedness?
questions.explanation: Convex lenses help focus light correctly for long-sighted eyes.

questions.question: Why do we see our image in a mirror?

options.A: Light is absorbed by the mirror
options.B: Light reflects from the mirror into our eyes

questions.explanation: Reflection of light from the mirror forms the image.

questions.question: Which property of light causes shadows to form?
questions.explanation: Shadows form because light travels in straight lines and is blocked by objects.

questions.question: Which mirror always produces upright and smaller images?
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors always produce upright and smaller images.

questions.question: What happens when light passes from air into water?
questions.explanation: Light bends when passing between different media. This is called refraction.

questions.question: Which instrument uses lenses to observe distant objects?
questions.explanation: Telescopes use lenses to observe distant celestial objects.

questions.question: Why are concave mirrors used as shaving mirrors?

options.B: They produce magnified images when the object is close

questions.explanation: Concave mirrors enlarge images when the object is close.

questions.question: Which lens spreads light rays outward?
questions.explanation: Concave lenses diverge light rays.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains lateral inversion?

options.B: Left appears right in a mirror image

questions.explanation: Lateral inversion is the sideways reversal of images in mirrors.

questions.question: Which device uses lenses to take photographs?
questions.explanation: Cameras use lenses to focus light and capture images.

questions.question: Which property of light allows us to see objects around us?
questions.explanation: Light reflects from objects and enters our eyes.

questions.question: Which optical device uses both mirrors and lenses?
questions.explanation: Many telescopes use mirrors and lenses to observe distant objects.

questions.question: Which mirror is used in solar cookers to concentrate sunlight?
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors focus sunlight to a point.

questions.question: Which device uses convex lenses to magnify very small objects?
questions.explanation: Microscopes use convex lenses for magnification.

questions.question: Which law states that the angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection?
questions.explanation: The law of reflection states that the angles of incidence and reflection are equal.

questions.question: Which mirror forms images behind the mirror surface?
questions.explanation: Both plane and convex mirrors form images behind the mirror.

questions.question: Which factor affects the brightness of an image in a mirror?
questions.explanation: More reflected light makes images appear brighter.

questions.question: Which phenomenon explains why a straw appears bent in water?
questions.explanation: Light bends when passing between air and water.

questions.question: Which type of mirror is most suitable for security mirrors in shops?
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors provide a wide field of view for security.
